# 制作过程

## Initial Design

DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>"},"isMeta":true,"uuid":"a7f206bb-3823-42e2-9ff1-da8dcb956b49","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:27:37.835Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main"}
{"parentUuid":"a7f206bb-3823-42e2-9ff1-da8dcb956b49","isSidechain":false,"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"<command-name>/model</command-name>\n            <command-message>model</command-message>\n            <command-args></command-args>"},"uuid":"114830d2-e8e4-4c11-a5b8-cffcc7f2a1f1","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:27:37.835Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main"}
{"parentUuid":"114830d2-e8e4-4c11-a5b8-cffcc7f2a1f1","isSidechain":false,"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"<local-command-stdout>Set model to \u001b[1mOpus 4.6 (1M context)\u001b[22m</local-command-stdout>"},"uuid":"070eaf3b-846a-4f99-888a-c55ab0e58e41","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:27:37.835Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main"}
{"type":"file-history-snapshot","messageId":"ffc0eaef-d224-49b8-b93f-817643f6b243","snapshot":{"messageId":"ffc0eaef-d224-49b8-b93f-817643f6b243","trackedFileBackups":{},"timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:43:19.582Z"},"isSnapshotUpdate":false}
{"parentUuid":"070eaf3b-846a-4f99-888a-c55ab0e58e41","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"9ec6ffc6-17fb-4d6f-9197-a90825d38591","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"我是一个大二上海交通大学ACM班的本科生，这学期修了CS2916大语言模型这门课，这门课要求进行一个长达45分钟的pre，以四人小组为单位，每个成员都要上台进行汇报，我们组的主题是自我提升与自我进化算法，我现在希望你对我们的pre进行设计，我给你几篇论文参考一下，V-STaR: Training Verifiers for Self-Taught Reasoners，Evolution through Large Models，你也可以自己找一些论文阅读，首先，列出pre的大纲，清晰详尽，接着请明确每位成员的分工，详细说明每位同学负责的内容。特别值得注意的是，你需要把演讲控制在45分钟左右，尽量做到互动性强，风格严谨但又不失幽默，通俗易懂。你面向的群体是一群对LLM 有一定了解但几乎不了解自我提升与自我进化算法的本科生，所以要注意pre的分寸。最后把计划写在根目录下的plan.md 里"},"uuid":"ffc0eaef-d224-49b8-b93f-817643f6b243","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:43:19.581Z","permissionMode":"default","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main"}
{"parentUuid":"ffc0eaef-d224-49b8-b93f-817643f6b243","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.\n- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.\nTRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.\nSKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- statusline: Set up Claude Code's status line UI\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch\n- insights: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions\n- team-onboarding: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage","skillCount":12,"isInitial":true},"type":"attachment","uuid":"387c1ef3-7af7-46fc-bc94-9dfc18529825","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:43:19.581Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main"}
{"parentUuid":"387c1ef3-7af7-46fc-bc94-9dfc18529825","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_01LszNnrbFTKewpf9fgGySGx","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The user is a sophomore at Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU) ACM class, taking a course CS2916 on Large Language Models. They need to design a 45-minute group presentation (4 members) on \"Self-Improving and Self-Evolving Algorithms.\" \n\nLet me first understand the project context by looking at the repository, then create a comprehensive presentation plan.\n\nLet me explore the repo first to understand what content is already available.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":29243,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":329,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":29243},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"type":"assistant","uuid":"d5005830-8946-4598-91b0-6b56a9debf3f","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:43:33.053Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main"}
{"parentUuid":"d5005830-8946-4598-91b0-6b56a9debf3f","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_01LszNnrbFTKewpf9fgGySGx","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Let me first explore the repository to understand what materials are already available, then design the presentation plan."}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":29243,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":329,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":29243},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"type":"assistant","uuid":"abba960c-8ad2-4f09-ba01-eb780b47d671","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:43:33.216Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main"}
{"parentUuid":"abba960c-8ad2-4f09-ba01-eb780b47d671","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_01LszNnrbFTKewpf9fgGySGx","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_bdrk_01D4G7FwArCBzN1XkRVtYVWe","name":"Agent","input":{"subagent_type":"Explore","description":"Explore repo structure","prompt":"Thoroughly explore the repository at /home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm. I need to understand:\n1. The overall structure and what files/directories exist\n2. Any existing documentation, papers, notes, or outlines\n3. Any code implementations related to self-improving or self-evolving algorithms\n4. b1]","is_error":false}]},"uuid":"7908f4b5-9822-4d4f-8ebb-d3b579c3a77d","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:43:43.157Z","toolUseResult":{"stdout":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/info/exclude\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/HEAD\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/description\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/update.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/applypatch-msg.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/pre-push.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/post-update.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample\n/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/.git/config","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"40cf0a37-adee-44cf-bf00-47ac79ce8644","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main"}
{"parentUuid":"7908f4b5-9822-4d4f-8ebb-d3b579c3a77d","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_019jb3AjxCMTjJ81iKn1hkMf","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_bdrk_01RQfwtoZerh3ph1vsz5v6gr","name":"Glob","input":{"pattern":"**/*","path":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":650,"cache_read_input_tokens":29637,"output_tokens":88,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":650},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"type":"assistant","uuid":"7ae15cd6-4772-425f-a874-79dcb3988c43","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:43:48.267Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}
{"parentUuid":"7ae15cd6-4772-425f-a874-79dcb3988c43","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"9ec6ffc6-17fb-4d6f-9197-a90825d38591","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01RQfwtoZerh3ph1vsz5v6gr","type":"tool_result","content":".git/info/exclude\n.git/description\n.git/hooks/update.sample\n.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample\n.git/hooks/applypatch-msg.sample\n.git/hooks/commit-msg.sample\n.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample\n.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample\n.git/hooks/pre-push.sample\n.git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample\n.git/hooks/pre-receive.sample\n.git/hooks/post-update.sample\n.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample\n.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample\n.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample\n.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample\n.git/HEAD\n.git/config"}]},"uuid":"0fe86cdd-0602-452f-acc8-3f6a7816c6a1","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:43:48.339Z","toolUseResult":{"filenames":[".git/info/exclude",".git/description",".git/hooks/update.sample",".git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample",".git/hooks/applypatch-msg.sample",".git/hooks/commit-msg.sample",".git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample",".git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample",".git/hooks/pre-push.sample",".git/hooks/fsmonitor-watchman.sample",".git/hooks/pre-receive.sample",".git/hooks/post-update.sample",".git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample",".git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample",".git/hooks/pre-commit.sample",".git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample",".git/HEAD",".git/config"],"durationMs":64,"numFiles":18,"truncated":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7ae15cd6-4772-425f-a874-79dcb3988c43","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}
{"parentUuid":"0fe86cdd-0602-452f-acc8-3f6a7816c6a1","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_01DkNugjSWah1ysTjv7PE8Cw","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The repo is essentially empty - just a fresh git init with no content files. I need to design the presentation plan from scratch based on my knowledge of self-improving and self-evolving algorithms in the context of LLMs.\n\nLet me think carefully about this topic and design a comprehensive, well-structured 45-minute presentation.\n\nThe topic is \"Self-Improving and Self-Evolving Algorithms\" (自我提升与自我进化算法) in the context of LLMs. This is a rich topic that covers several important research directions:\n\n1. **Self-Improving LLMs**: Methods where LLMs improve their own performance through self-generated data, self-play, self-refinement, etc.\n   - Self-Play (e.g., Self-Play Fine-Tuning / SPIN)\n   - Self-Instruct\n   - Self-Refine / Iterative Refinement\n   - Constitutional AI (self-critique and revision)\n   - STaR (Self-Taught Reasoner)\n   - ReST (Reinforced Self-Training)\n\n2. **Self-Evolving Algorithms**: Methods where the algorithm itself evolves or adapts, including approaches like Evolution of Thought, EvoPrompt, OpenELM, and FunSearch where LLMs are used to discover novel algorithms and solutions.\n\nNow I'm pulling together the key papers that support these approaches—foundational work from Zelikman, Wang, Madaan, and others showing how models can bootstrap their own reasoning, refine outputs through self-critique, and improve through reinforcement learning without external supervision.\n\nFor the presentation structure, I'm dividing this into four segments across the team: starting with motivation and context for undergrads, then moving through the core self-improvement techniques, followed by more advanced self-evolving methods, and wrapping up with practical applications and open questions.\n\nI want to make this engaging for the audience, so I'm thinking about how to incorporate interactive elements like polls or quick quizzes, use relatable analogies, and build the complexity gradually from foundational concepts to cutting-edge approaches. The flow should feel natural rather than just lecturing through papers. 现在我在思考第四部分的具体内容——需要讨论自我提升面临的核心挑战，比如模型坍缩和奖励欺骗，然后探讨理论上界在哪里，最后连接到更广泛的AI安全和对齐问题。我还想找到一个贯穿全场的好类比，比如把LLM的自我提升比作没有老师的学生自学，这样能让观众更容易理解整个概念框架。\n\n现在我准备用中文写出详细的演讲计划，保留技术术语用英文。","signature":""}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":288,"cache_read_input_tokens":30287,"output_tokens":9110,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":288},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"type":"assistant","uuid":"f5b1738c-3cd9-4a7b-acc1-e5ec198dab78","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:44:29.378Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}
{"parentUuid":"f5b1738c-3cd9-4a7b-acc1-e5ec198dab78","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_01DkNugjSWah1ysTjv7PE8Cw","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Repo 目前是空的（刚 `git init`）。我来根据这个主题从头设计一份详尽的 presentation 计划。"}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":288,"cache_read_input_tokens":30287,"output_tokens":9110,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":288},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"type":"assistant","uuid":"1c25fbad-a5aa-4aef-93c5-1e708a536bbc","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:44:29.546Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}
{"parentUuid":"1c25fbad-a5aa-4aef-93c5-1e708a536bbc","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_01DkNugjSWah1ysTjv7PE8Cw","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_bdrk_01RP5rV5BhZPjdiqpvHhNzjd","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/plan.md","content":"# CS2916 Presentation Plan: 自我提升与自我进化算法\n\n> **课程**: CS2916 大语言模型 | **时长**: 45 分钟 | **人数**: 4 人\n\n---\n\n## 一、核心叙事线\n\n整场演讲围绕一个核心类比展开：\n\n> **LLM 的成长路径，就像一个学生从\"跟老师学\"到\"自己学\"的过程。**\n>\n> Pre-training = 读教科书；SFT/RLHF = 老师批改作业；\n> **Self-Improving = 自己出题、自己做、自己批、自己改。**\n\n四位成员依次回答四个递进问题：\n1. **为什么要自我提升？**（动机与全景）\n2. **怎么自己学？**（核心自我提升方法）\n3. **怎么自己进化？**（自我博弈与进化算法）\n4. **能学到什么程度？有什么风险？**（理论边界、挑战与未来）\n\n---\n\n## 二、详细大纲\n\n### Part 1：开场 —— 为什么 LLM 需要\"自学\"？（成员 A，约 10 分钟）\n\n| 时间 | 内容 | 备注 |\n|------|------|------|\n| 0:00-1:30 | **Hook：互动投票** | 用举手/弹幕提问：\"你觉得 GPT-4 级别的模型背后需要多少人工标注数据？\" 给出选项 A/B/C/D，揭晓答案后引出人工标注的高成本问题 |\n| 1:30-3:30 | **瓶颈：人工标注不可持续** | 数据标注成本曲线（配图表）；RLHF 需要大量人类偏好数据；高质量标注者本身就很稀缺（\"你去哪找 10 万个数学博士来标数据？\"——幽默点） |\n| 3:30-5:30 | **核心思想：Bootstrap（自举）** | 用学生自学的类比讲清楚\"用自己的输出来训练自己\"这个看似矛盾的想法；引用 Baron Munchausen 自己拽头发把自己从沼泽里拉出来的故事（Self-improvement 文献中的经典典故） |\n| 5:30-8:00 | **全景地图：分类框架** | 展示一张精心设计的分类图，把接下来要讲的所有方法放在统一框架中：<br>- **自我提升（Self-Improving）**：Self-Instruct, STaR, Self-Refine, ReST<br>- **自我博弈（Self-Play）**：SPIN, Self-Rewarding LM<br>- **自我进化（Self-Evolving）**：Constitutional AI, FunSearch, EvoPrompt<br>清晰定义三者的区别和联系 |\n| 8:00-10:00 | **关键问题预告 + 过渡** | \"一个模型真的能通过自己训练自己变得更强吗？这难道不是'自己批改自己的作业'吗？带着这个疑问，让我们来看看具体的方法。\" 交棒给成员 B |\n\n**成员 A 需要准备的材料**：\n- 一张人工标注成本的信息图\n- 自举（Bootstrap）的直觉动画/示意图\n- 方法全景分类图（这张图贯穿全场，后续成员讲到哪个方法就高亮哪个位置）\n- 互动投票的选项设计\n\n---\n\n### Part 2：核心方法 —— 自己出题，自己做，自己改（成员 B，约 12 分钟）\n\n| 时间 | 内容 | 备注 |\n|------|------|------|\n| 10:00-13:00 | **Self-Instruct：自己给自己出题** | 核心流程图：Seed Tasks → LLM 生成 Instruction → LLM 生成 Response → 过滤 → 微调<br>关键论文：Wang et al., 2022 \"Self-Instruct\"<br>强调\"种子任务\"的重要性——你不是从零开始，你需要一点火种 |\n| 13:00-16:30 | **STaR：自举推理能力** | 核心思想：让模型先尝试解题，答对了的 rationale 拿来当训练数据<br>关键创新：rationalization——答错的题，给它看答案，让它\"事后解释\"，这个解释也能拿来训练<br>论文：Zelikman et al., 2022 \"STaR: Bootstrapping Reasoning With Reasoning\"<br>用一个具体的数学推理例子走一遍流程<br>（幽默点：\"就像考试不会做的题，看了答案之后觉得'啊我本来也想到了'——STaR 就是把这个过程形式化了\"） |\n| 16:30-19:00 | **Self-Refine：迭代自我批评与修改** | 三步循环：Generate → Critique → Refine<br>关键点：不需要微调，纯 prompting<br>论文：Madaan et al., 2023 \"Self-Refine\"<br>**现场互动**：请一位同学给出一段\"很烂的代码\"（或者提前准备），现场用 ChatGPT 演示 Self-Refine 过程：先写→自评→改进，让观众直观感受迭代改进的效果 |\n| 19:00-22:00 | **ReST / ReST^EM：强化自训练** | 核心思想：Generate（采样多个解）→ Improve（用奖励模型筛选）→ Train（用好的样本微调）<br>与 STaR 的区别：引入了显式的奖励信号，更接近 RL 的框架<br>论文：Gulcehre et al., 2023; Singh et al., 2023<br>一张对比表：Self-Instruct vs STaR vs Self-Refine vs ReST 的异同 |\n\n**过渡**（22:00）：\n> \"到目前为止，我们看到的方法有一个共同特点：模型在 **单向地** 提升自己。但如果模型能和自己对弈呢？如果模型能自己当裁判呢？甚至，如果我们用进化的方式让模型群体自己'演化'呢？\"\n\n**成员 B 需要准备的材料**：\n- 每个方法的流程图（统一视觉风格）\n- STaR 的数学推理具体示例（step-by-step）\n- Self-Refine 的现场 demo（提前测试好，确保能跑通）\n- 四种方法的对比表\n\n---\n\n### Part 3：自我博弈与自我进化 —— 当模型自己和自己下棋（成员 C，约 12 分钟）\n\n| 时间 | 内容 | 备注 |\n|------|------|------|\n| 22:00-25:00 | **SPIN：自我博弈微调** | 核心思想：类比 AlphaGo 的自我博弈——主模型 vs. 上一轮的自己<br>目标：让当前模型的输出与人类数据不可区分<br>论文：Chen et al., 2024 \"Self-Play Fine-Tuning Converts Weak Language Models to Strong Language Models\"<br>数学直觉（不要太深，给一个 min-max 的大致概念就好）<br>关键结果：Zephyr-7B 在 SPIN 后超越了 GPT-4 在部分 benchmark 上的表现 |\n| 25:00-28:30 | **Self-Rewarding Language Models：自己当裁判** | 核心突破：不再需要单独的奖励模型（Reward Model），LLM 自己既当选手又当裁判<br>迭代流程：用自己生成 + 自己评分 → 筛选 → 微调 → 新一轮<br>论文：Yuan et al., 2024<br>与 RLHF 的关键区别对比图<br>（幽默点：\"这就像你自己给自己的论文打分——听起来很不靠谱，但意外地 work\"） |\n| 28:30-31:00 | **Constitutional AI：给自我进化加上\"宪法\"** | 核心思想：用一组人写的原则（Constitution）代替大量人工标注<br>两阶段：Critique → Revision（基于原则的自我批评）→ RLAIF（AI 反馈的强化学习）<br>论文：Bai et al., 2022<br>强调这是 Anthropic 的核心方法论——与安全对齐的紧密联系 |\n| 31:00-34:00 | **进化算法 × LLM：FunSearch & EvoPrompt** | FunSearch（DeepMind, 2024）：用 LLM 做进化算法的 mutation operator，发现了超越人类已知最优解的数学构造<br>EvoPrompt：用进化算法优化 prompt<br>核心洞察：LLM 不只是被进化的对象，它本身就是进化过程中的\"变异引擎\"<br>**小互动/Quiz**：\"以下哪个是 FunSearch 真正发现的？\" A. 新的排序算法 B. cap set 问题的新下界 C. 新的蛋白质结构 → 揭晓答案并简述 |\n\n**过渡**（34:00）：\n> \"听到这里，你可能会觉得 Self-Improvement 是一个银弹——模型自己就能越变越强。但事情真的这么美好吗？\"\n\n**成员 C 需要准备的材料**：\n- SPIN 的自我博弈对比图（类比 AlphaGo）\n- Self-Rewarding vs RLHF 的对比图\n- Constitutional AI 的\"宪法原则\"示例\n- FunSearch 的结果展示（cap set 问题的图示）\n- 互动 Quiz 的设计\n\n---\n\n### Part 4：天花板、陷阱与未来 —— 自我提升的边界在哪？（成员 D，约 11 分钟）\n\n| 时间 | 内容 | 备注 |\n|------|------|------|\n| 34:00-37:00 | **三大核心挑战** | **1. 模型坍缩（Model Collapse）**：用自己的输出训自己 → 分布越来越窄 → 多样性丧失 → 质量下降（配图：Shumailov et al., 2023 的实验曲线）<br>**2. 奖励欺骗（Reward Hacking）**：Self-Rewarding 的风险——模型学会给自己\"放水\"<br>**3. 分布偏移（Distribution Shift）**：生成数据偏离真实数据分布<br>用一个统一的例子把三个问题串起来 |\n| 37:00-39:30 | **理论视角：自我提升的上界** | 直觉问题：\"一个 60 分的模型能把自己训练到 90 分吗？\"<br>Huang et al., 2022 \"Large Language Models Can Self-Improve\" 的关键发现：模型规模足够大时，Self-Consistency + CoT 就能自我提升<br>但有条件的：需要 **足够的多样性** 和 **可靠的验证信号**<br>Scaling 的视角：数据 scaling vs. 计算 scaling vs. self-improvement scaling |\n| 39:30-42:00 | **前沿展望（2024-2025）** | 1. **Test-Time Compute Scaling**：o1/o3 的思路——不只在训练时提升，推理时也能\"自我提升\"<br>2. **Multi-Agent Self-Improvement**：多个模型互相提升（debate, collaboration）<br>3. **Self-Improving Agents**：不只是语言能力，而是在环境中行动并从反馈中学习<br>4. **与 AI Safety 的关系**：递归自我提升（Recursive Self-Improvement）是 AI 安全研究的核心关切之一 |\n| 42:00-44:00 | **总结与回顾** | 回到开场的全景地图，把所有方法点亮<br>一句话总结每个 Part 的核心 takeaway<br>回答开场的问题：\"模型真的能自己批改自己的作业吗？\" → \"可以，但需要精心设计的机制来避免'自欺欺人'\"<br>留一个开放性思考：\"如果模型真的能无限自我提升，那人类标注者的角色会变成什么？\" |\n| 44:00-45:00 | **Q&A 引导** | 抛出 2-3 个引导性问题帮助观众提问<br>如时间允许，回答 1-2 个问题 |\n\n**成员 D 需要准备的材料**：\n- Model Collapse 的实验曲线图\n- 理论上界的直觉图示\n- 前沿方向的 timeline 图\n- 全场总结的回顾 slide（重新展示全景图并高亮所有已讲内容）\n\n---\n\n## 三、成员分工明细\n\n### 成员 A：开场与背景（负责 Part 1）\n\n**演讲内容**：0:00 - 10:00（约 10 分钟）\n\n| 职责 | 具体任务 |\n|------|----------|\n| **演讲** | 负责开场 Hook（互动投票）、瓶颈分析、Bootstrap 核心思想、全景分类图讲解 |\n| **PPT 制作** | 制作 Part 1 的 slides（约 10-12 页）；**重点制作全景分类图**（这张图被全场复用，需要美观清晰） |\n| **内容研究** | 调研人工标注成本数据（InstructGPT, RLHF 的标注规模）；整理 Self-Improving 领域的综述性材料 |\n| **全场职责** | 负责 **统一 PPT 风格模板**（配色、字体、排版），确保四位成员的 slides 视觉一致 |\n\n**关键论文/资料**：\n- Ouyang et al., 2022 \"Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback\"（InstructGPT，了解 RLHF 的标注成本）\n- 一篇 Self-Improvement 的 survey paper 作为全景框架参考\n\n---\n\n### 成员 B：核心自我提升方法（负责 Part 2）\n\n**演讲内容**：10:00 - 22:00（约 12 分钟）\n\n| 职责 | 具体任务 |\n|------|----------|\n| **演讲** | 讲解 Self-Instruct、STaR、Self-Refine、ReST 四种方法；负责现场 Self-Refine demo |\n| **PPT 制作** | 制作 Part 2 的 slides（约 12-15 页）；每种方法一张清晰的流程图 |\n| **内容研究** | 深读四篇核心论文，提取核心算法流程和关键实验结果 |\n| **Demo 准备** | 准备 Self-Refine 的现场演示（可以用 ChatGPT/Claude API，提前准备好 fallback 截图以防网络问题） |\n| **全场职责** | 制作 **四种方法的对比表**（输入、是否需要微调、是否需要奖励模型、适用场景等维度） |\n\n**关键论文**：\n- Wang et al., 2022 \"Self-Instruct: Aligning Language Models with Self-Generated Instructions\"\n- Zelikman et al., 2022 \"STaR: Bootstrapping Reasoning With Reasoning\"\n- Madaan et al., 2023 \"Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback\"\n- Gulcehre et al., 2023 \"Reinforced Self-Training (ReST) for Language Modeling\"\n- Singh et al., 2023 \"Beyond Human Data: Scaling Self-Training for Problem-Solving with Language Models\"\n\n---\n\n### 成员 C：自我博弈与自我进化（负责 Part 3）\n\n**演讲内容**：22:00 - 34:00（约 12 分钟）\n\n| 职责 | 具体任务 |\n|------|----------|\n| **演讲** | 讲解 SPIN、Self-Rewarding LM、Constitutional AI、FunSearch/EvoPrompt；负责 Quiz 互动 |\n| **PPT 制作** | 制作 Part 3 的 slides（约 12-15 页）；SPIN 的博弈示意图、Self-Rewarding vs RLHF 对比图 |\n| **内容研究** | 深读相关论文，特别理解 SPIN 的博弈论直觉和 Constitutional AI 的设计哲学 |\n| **互动设计** | 设计 FunSearch 相关的 Quiz 问题 |\n| **全场职责** | 负责收集和整理所有引用的 **参考文献列表**，放在最后的 References slide 中 |\n\n**关键论文**：\n- Chen et al., 2024 \"Self-Play Fine-Tuning Converts Weak Language Models to Strong Language Models\"\n- Yuan et al., 2024 \"Self-Rewarding Language Models\"\n- Bai et al., 2022 \"Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback\"\n- Romera-Paredes et al., 2024 \"Mathematical discoveries from program search with large language models\"（FunSearch）\n- Guo et al., 2023 \"Connecting Large Language Models with Evolutionary Algorithms Yields Powerful Prompt Optimizers\"（EvoPrompt）\n\n---\n\n### 成员 D：挑战、理论与未来（负责 Part 4）\n\n**演讲内容**：34:00 - 45:00（约 11 分钟）\n\n| 职责 | 具体任务 |\n|------|----------|\n| **演讲** | 讲解三大挑战、理论上界、前沿展望、全场总结；引导 Q&A |\n| **PPT 制作** | 制作 Part 4 的 slides（约 10-12 页）；Model Collapse 实验图、总结回顾 slide |\n| **内容研究** | 深读 Model Collapse、理论分析、Test-Time Compute 等相关论文 |\n| **Q&A 准备** | 提前准备 5-8 个可能的 Q&A 问题及简要回答，与全组共享 |\n| **全场职责** | 负责 **排练统筹**（组织至少 2 次完整排练，记录时间，确保不超时） |\n\n**关键论文**：\n- Shumailov et al., 2023 \"The Curse of Recursion: Training on Generated Data Makes Models Forget\"\n- Huang et al., 2022 \"Large Language Models Can Self-Improve\"\n- Snell et al., 2024 \"Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute\"\n- Hosseini et al., 2024 \"V-STaR: Training Verifiers for Self-Taught Reasoners\"\n\n---\n\n## 四、互动环节设计\n\n| 时间点 | 互动形式 | 负责人 | 说明 |\n|--------|----------|--------|------|\n| 0:00-1:30 | **举手投票** | 成员 A | \"你觉得训练一个 ChatGPT 级别的模型需要多少人工标注？\" |\n| 16:30-19:00 | **现场 Demo** | 成员 B | Self-Refine 演示：给模型一个任务，让它自我批评并改进 |\n| 31:00-34:00 | **Quiz 抢答** | 成员 C | FunSearch 相关的选择题，答对的同学可以获得小奖品（可选） |\n| 42:00-44:00 | **开放讨论** | 成员 D | 总结时的思考题，引导 Q&A |\n\n---\n\n## 五、PPT 结构建议\n\n```\nSlide 0   : 封面（标题、课程信息、组员信息）         [成员 A 制作]\nSlide 1-10: Part 1 - 动机与全景                       [成员 A 制作]\nSlide 11-25: Part 2 - 核心自我提升方法                 [成员 B 制作]\nSlide 26-40: Part 3 - 自我博弈与自我进化               [成员 C 制作]\nSlide 41-52: Part 4 - 挑战、展望与总结                 [成员 D 制作]\nSlide 53  : References                                 [成员 C 汇总]\nSlide 54  : Thank You / Q&A                            [成员 D 制作]\n```\n\n**视觉风格建议**：\n- 主色调：深蓝 + 白色 + 亮橙色（高亮重点用）\n- 字体：英文用 Helvetica/Arial，中文用思源黑体\n- 流程图风格统一，建议用同一套图标库\n- 每张 slide 的文字不超过 5 行，重点用图表和动画表达\n- 全景分类图在每个 Part 开始时复现，高亮当前讲解的部分（\"你在这里\"效果）\n\n---\n\n## 六、时间轴总览\n\n```\n 0:00 ──── 10:00 ──── 22:00 ──── 34:00 ──── 45:00\n │  成员 A  │  成员 B   │  成员 C   │  成员 D  │\n │  10 min  │  12 min   │  12 min   │  11 min  │\n │          │           │           │          │\n │ 动机     │ 核心方法   │ 博弈+进化  │ 挑战+未来 │\n │ +全景图  │ +Demo     │ +Quiz     │ +总结+QA │\n```\n\n---\n\n## 七、排练计划\n\n| 次数 | 时间建议 | 重点 |\n|------|----------|------|\n| **第 1 次** | 正式前 7-10 天 | 每人只讲大纲，确认内容覆盖无遗漏、无重复，调整时间分配 |\n| **第 2 次** | 正式前 3-4 天 | 完整 PPT 排练，严格计时，练习过渡语，测试 Demo |\n| **第 3 次**（可选）| 正式前 1 天 | 精简版快速过一遍，重点练薄弱环节和 Q&A 应对 |\n\n---\n\n## 八、风险与备案\n\n| 风险 | 备案 |\n|------|------|\n| Demo 现场翻车（网络问题/API 不响应） | 提前录好 Demo 视频 + 截图，可以无缝切换 |\n| 某位成员超时 | 每人 PPT 中标记\"可跳过\"的 slide，排练时练习压缩版 |\n| Q&A 冷场 | 成员 D 准备 2-3 个引导性问题（\"有同学想问 xxx 吗？\"） |\n| 观众对数学公式失去兴趣 | 所有公式都配直觉解释和类比，绝不出现\"这个公式很显然\" |\n\n---\n\n## 九、核心参考文献\n\n1. Wang et al., 2022. *Self-Instruct: Aligning Language Models with Self-Generated Instructions*\n2. Zelikman et al., 2022. *STaR: Bootstrapping Reasoning With Reasoning*\n3. Madaan et al., 2023. *Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback*\n4. Gulcehre et al., 2023. *Reinforced Self-Training (ReST) for Language Modeling*\n5. Chen et al., 2024. *Self-Play Fine-Tuning Converts Weak Language Models to Strong Language Models*\n6. Yuan et al., 2024. *Self-Rewarding Language Models*\n7. Bai et al., 2022. *Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback*\n8. Romera-Paredes et al., 2024. *Mathematical Discoveries from Program Search with Large Language Models* (FunSearch)\n9. Guo et al., 2023. *Connecting Large Language Models with Evolutionary Algorithms Yields Powerful Prompt Optimizers* (EvoPrompt)\n10. Shumailov et al., 2023. *The Curse of Recursion: Training on Generated Data Makes Models Forget*\n11. Huang et al., 2022. *Large Language Models Can Self-Improve*\n12. Snell et al., 2024. *Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute Optimally can be More Effective than Scaling Model Parameters*\n13. Singh et al., 2023. *Beyond Human Data: Scaling Self-Training for Problem-Solving with Language Models*\n14. Hosseini et al., 2024. *V-STaR: Training Verifiers for Self-Taught Reasoners*\n"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":288,"cache_read_input_tokens":30287,"output_tokens":9110,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":288},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"type":"assistant","uuid":"c522d9a2-1f46-4c3f-aefc-f96f34cc9a87","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:46:40.013Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}
{"type":"file-history-snapshot","messageId":"c522d9a2-1f46-4c3f-aefc-f96f34cc9a87","snapshot":{"messageId":"ffc0eaef-d224-49b8-b93f-817643f6b243","trackedFileBackups":{"plan.md":{"backupFileName":null,"version":1,"backupTime":"2026-04-21T17:46:43.909Z"}},"timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:43:19.582Z"},"isSnapshotUpdate":true}
{"parentUuid":"c522d9a2-1f46-4c3f-aefc-f96f34cc9a87","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"9ec6ffc6-17fb-4d6f-9197-a90825d38591","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01RP5rV5BhZPjdiqpvHhNzjd","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/plan.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"uuid":"cc41117b-490a-41de-b61c-bc405f73f239","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:46:43.930Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"create","filePath":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/plan.md","content":"# CS2916 Presentation Plan: 自我提升与自我进化算法\n\n> **课程**: CS2916 大语言模型 | **时长**: 45 分钟 | **人数**: 4 人\n\n---\n\n## 一、核心叙事线\n\n整场演讲围绕一个核心类比展开：\n\n> **LLM 的成长路径，就像一个学生从\"跟老师学\"到\"自己学\"的过程。**\n>\n> Pre-training = 读教科书；SFT/RLHF = 老师批改作业；\n> **Self-Improving = 自己出题、自己做、自己批、自己改。**\n\n四位成员依次回答四个递进问题：\n1. **为什么要自我提升？**（动机与全景）\n2. **怎么自己学？**（核心自我提升方法）\n3. **怎么自己进化？**（自我博弈与进化算法）\n4. **能学到什么程度？有什么风险？**（理论边界、挑战与未来）\n\n---\n\n## 二、详细大纲\n\n### Part 1：开场 —— 为什么 LLM 需要\"自学\"？（成员 A，约 10 分钟）\n\n| 时间 | 内容 | 备注 |\n|------|------|------|\n| 0:00-1:30 | **Hook：互动投票** | 用举手/弹幕提问：\"你觉得 GPT-4 级别的模型背后需要多少人工标注数据？\" 给出选项 A/B/C/D，揭晓答案后引出人工标注的高成本问题 |\n| 1:30-3:30 | **瓶颈：人工标注不可持续** | 数据标注成本曲线（配图表）；RLHF 需要大量人类偏好数据；高质量标注者本身就很稀缺（\"你去哪找 10 万个数学博士来标数据？\"——幽默点） |\n| 3:30-5:30 | **核心思想：Bootstrap（自举）** | 用学生自学的类比讲清楚\"用自己的输出来训练自己\"这个看似矛盾的想法；引用 Baron Munchausen 自己拽头发把自己从沼泽里拉出来的故事（Self-improvement 文献中的经典典故） |\n| 5:30-8:00 | **全景地图：分类框架** | 展示一张精心设计的分类图，把接下来要讲的所有方法放在统一框架中：<br>- **自我提升（Self-Improving）**：Self-Instruct, STaR, Self-Refine, ReST<br>- **自我博弈（Self-Play）**：SPIN, Self-Rewarding LM<br>- **自我进化（Self-Evolving）**：Constitutional AI, FunSearch, EvoPrompt<br>清晰定义三者的区别和联系 |\n| 8:00-10:00 | **关键问题预告 + 过渡** | \"一个模型真的能通过自己训练自己变得更强吗？这难道不是'自己批改自己的作业'吗？带着这个疑问，让我们来看看具体的方法。\" 交棒给成员 B |\n\n**成员 A 需要准备的材料**：\n- 一张人工标注成本的信息图\n- 自举（Bootstrap）的直觉动画/示意图\n- 方法全景分类图（这张图贯穿全场，后续成员讲到哪个方法就高亮哪个位置）\n- 互动投票的选项设计\n\n---\n\n### Part 2：核心方法 —— 自己出题，自己做，自己改（成员 B，约 12 分钟）\n\n| 时间 | 内容 | 备注 |\n|------|------|------|\n| 10:00-13:00 | **Self-Instruct：自己给自己出题** | 核心流程图：Seed Tasks → LLM 生成 Instruction → LLM 生成 Response → 过滤 → 微调<br>关键论文：Wang et al., 2022 \"Self-Instruct\"<br>强调\"种子任务\"的重要性——你不是从零开始，你需要一点火种 |\n| 13:00-16:30 | **STaR：自举推理能力** | 核心思想：让模型先尝试解题，答对了的 rationale 拿来当训练数据<br>关键创新：rationalization——答错的题，给它看答案，让它\"事后解释\"，这个解释也能拿来训练<br>论文：Zelikman et al., 2022 \"STaR: Bootstrapping Reasoning With Reasoning\"<br>用一个具体的数学推理例子走一遍流程<br>（幽默点：\"就像考试不会做的题，看了答案之后觉得'啊我本来也想到了'——STaR 就是把这个过程形式化了\"） |\n| 16:30-19:00 | **Self-Refine：迭代自我批评与修改** | 三步循环：Generate → Critique → Refine<br>关键点：不需要微调，纯 prompting<br>论文：Madaan et al., 2023 \"Self-Refine\"<br>**现场互动**：请一位同学给出一段\"很烂的代码\"（或者提前准备），现场用 ChatGPT 演示 Self-Refine 过程：先写→自评→改进，让观众直观感受迭代改进的效果 |\n| 19:00-22:00 | **ReST / ReST^EM：强化自训练** | 核心思想：Generate（采样多个解）→ Improve（用奖励模型筛选）→ Train（用好的样本微调）<br>与 STaR 的区别：引入了显式的奖励信号，更接近 RL 的框架<br>论文：Gulcehre et al., 2023; Singh et al., 2023<br>一张对比表：Self-Instruct vs STaR vs Self-Refine vs ReST 的异同 |\n\n**过渡**（22:00）：\n> \"到目前为止，我们看到的方法有一个共同特点：模型在 **单向地** 提升自己。但如果模型能和自己对弈呢？如果模型能自己当裁判呢？甚至，如果我们用进化的方式让模型群体自己'演化'呢？\"\n\n**成员 B 需要准备的材料**：\n- 每个方法的流程图（统一视觉风格）\n- STaR 的数学推理具体示例（step-by-step）\n- Self-Refine 的现场 demo（提前测试好，确保能跑通）\n- 四种方法的对比表\n\n---\n\n### Part 3：自我博弈与自我进化 —— 当模型自己和自己下棋（成员 C，约 12 分钟）\n\n| 时间 | 内容 | 备注 |\n|------|------|------|\n| 22:00-25:00 | **SPIN：自我博弈微调** | 核心思想：类比 AlphaGo 的自我博弈——主模型 vs. 上一轮的自己<br>目标：让当前模型的输出与人类数据不可区分<br>论文：Chen et al., 2024 \"Self-Play Fine-Tuning Converts Weak Language Models to Strong Language Models\"<br>数学直觉（不要太深，给一个 min-max 的大致概念就好）<br>关键结果：Zephyr-7B 在 SPIN 后超越了 GPT-4 在部分 benchmark 上的表现 |\n| 25:00-28:30 | **Self-Rewarding Language Models：自己当裁判** | 核心突破：不再需要单独的奖励模型（Reward Model），LLM 自己既当选手又当裁判<br>迭代流程：用自己生成 + 自己评分 → 筛选 → 微调 → 新一轮<br>论文：Yuan et al., 2024<br>与 RLHF 的关键区别对比图<br>（幽默点：\"这就像你自己给自己的论文打分——听起来很不靠谱，但意外地 work\"） |\n| 28:30-31:00 | **Constitutional AI：给自我进化加上\"宪法\"** | 核心思想：用一组人写的原则（Constitution）代替大量人工标注<br>两阶段：Critique → Revision（基于原则的自我批评）→ RLAIF（AI 反馈的强化学习）<br>论文：Bai et al., 2022<br>强调这是 Anthropic 的核心方法论——与安全对齐的紧密联系 |\n| 31:00-34:00 | **进化算法 × LLM：FunSearch & EvoPrompt** | FunSearch（DeepMind, 2024）：用 LLM 做进化算法的 mutation operator，发现了超越人类已知最优解的数学构造<br>EvoPrompt：用进化算法优化 prompt<br>核心洞察：LLM 不只是被进化的对象，它本身就是进化过程中的\"变异引擎\"<br>**小互动/Quiz**：\"以下哪个是 FunSearch 真正发现的？\" A. 新的排序算法 B. cap set 问题的新下界 C. 新的蛋白质结构 → 揭晓答案并简述 |\n\n**过渡**（34:00）：\n> \"听到这里，你可能会觉得 Self-Improvement 是一个银弹——模型自己就能越变越强。但事情真的这么美好吗？\"\n\n**成员 C 需要准备的材料**：\n- SPIN 的自我博弈对比图（类比 AlphaGo）\n- Self-Rewarding vs RLHF 的对比图\n- Constitutional AI 的\"宪法原则\"示例\n- FunSearch 的结果展示（cap set 问题的图示）\n- 互动 Quiz 的设计\n\n---\n\n### Part 4：天花板、陷阱与未来 —— 自我提升的边界在哪？（成员 D，约 11 分钟）\n\n| 时间 | 内容 | 备注 |\n|------|------|------|\n| 34:00-37:00 | **三大核心挑战** | **1. 模型坍缩（Model Collapse）**：用自己的输出训自己 → 分布越来越窄 → 多样性丧失 → 质量下降（配图：Shumailov et al., 2023 的实验曲线）<br>**2. 奖励欺骗（Reward Hacking）**：Self-Rewarding 的风险——模型学会给自己\"放水\"<br>**3. 分布偏移（Distribution Shift）**：生成数据偏离真实数据分布<br>用一个统一的例子把三个问题串起来 |\n| 37:00-39:30 | **理论视角：自我提升的上界** | 直觉问题：\"一个 60 分的模型能把自己训练到 90 分吗？\"<br>Huang et al., 2022 \"Large Language Models Can Self-Improve\" 的关键发现：模型规模足够大时，Self-Consistency + CoT 就能自我提升<br>但有条件的：需要 **足够的多样性** 和 **可靠的验证信号**<br>Scaling 的视角：数据 scaling vs. 计算 scaling vs. self-improvement scaling |\n| 39:30-42:00 | **前沿展望（2024-2025）** | 1. **Test-Time Compute Scaling**：o1/o3 的思路——不只在训练时提升，推理时也能\"自我提升\"<br>2. **Multi-Agent Self-Improvement**：多个模型互相提升（debate, collaboration）<br>3. **Self-Improving Agents**：不只是语言能力，而是在环境中行动并从反馈中学习<br>4. **与 AI Safety 的关系**：递归自我提升（Recursive Self-Improvement）是 AI 安全研究的核心关切之一 |\n| 42:00-44:00 | **总结与回顾** | 回到开场的全景地图，把所有方法点亮<br>一句话总结每个 Part 的核心 takeaway<br>回答开场的问题：\"模型真的能自己批改自己的作业吗？\" → \"可以，但需要精心设计的机制来避免'自欺欺人'\"<br>留一个开放性思考：\"如果模型真的能无限自我提升，那人类标注者的角色会变成什么？\" |\n| 44:00-45:00 | **Q&A 引导** | 抛出 2-3 个引导性问题帮助观众提问<br>如时间允许，回答 1-2 个问题 |\n\n**成员 D 需要准备的材料**：\n- Model Collapse 的实验曲线图\n- 理论上界的直觉图示\n- 前沿方向的 timeline 图\n- 全场总结的回顾 slide（重新展示全景图并高亮所有已讲内容）\n\n---\n\n## 三、成员分工明细\n\n### 成员 A：开场与背景（负责 Part 1）\n\n**演讲内容**：0:00 - 10:00（约 10 分钟）\n\n| 职责 | 具体任务 |\n|------|----------|\n| **演讲** | 负责开场 Hook（互动投票）、瓶颈分析、Bootstrap 核心思想、全景分类图讲解 |\n| **PPT 制作** | 制作 Part 1 的 slides（约 10-12 页）；**重点制作全景分类图**（这张图被全场复用，需要美观清晰） |\n| **内容研究** | 调研人工标注成本数据（InstructGPT, RLHF 的标注规模）；整理 Self-Improving 领域的综述性材料 |\n| **全场职责** | 负责 **统一 PPT 风格模板**（配色、字体、排版），确保四位成员的 slides 视觉一致 |\n\n**关键论文/资料**：\n- Ouyang et al., 2022 \"Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback\"（InstructGPT，了解 RLHF 的标注成本）\n- 一篇 Self-Improvement 的 survey paper 作为全景框架参考\n\n---\n\n### 成员 B：核心自我提升方法（负责 Part 2）\n\n**演讲内容**：10:00 - 22:00（约 12 分钟）\n\n| 职责 | 具体任务 |\n|------|----------|\n| **演讲** | 讲解 Self-Instruct、STaR、Self-Refine、ReST 四种方法；负责现场 Self-Refine demo |\n| **PPT 制作** | 制作 Part 2 的 slides（约 12-15 页）；每种方法一张清晰的流程图 |\n| **内容研究** | 深读四篇核心论文，提取核心算法流程和关键实验结果 |\n| **Demo 准备** | 准备 Self-Refine 的现场演示（可以用 ChatGPT/Claude API，提前准备好 fallback 截图以防网络问题） |\n| **全场职责** | 制作 **四种方法的对比表**（输入、是否需要微调、是否需要奖励模型、适用场景等维度） |\n\n**关键论文**：\n- Wang et al., 2022 \"Self-Instruct: Aligning Language Models with Self-Generated Instructions\"\n- Zelikman et al., 2022 \"STaR: Bootstrapping Reasoning With Reasoning\"\n- Madaan et al., 2023 \"Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback\"\n- Gulcehre et al., 2023 \"Reinforced Self-Training (ReST) for Language Modeling\"\n- Singh et al., 2023 \"Beyond Human Data: Scaling Self-Training for Problem-Solving with Language Models\"\n\n---\n\n### 成员 C：自我博弈与自我进化（负责 Part 3）\n\n**演讲内容**：22:00 - 34:00（约 12 分钟）\n\n| 职责 | 具体任务 |\n|------|----------|\n| **演讲** | 讲解 SPIN、Self-Rewarding LM、Constitutional AI、FunSearch/EvoPrompt；负责 Quiz 互动 |\n| **PPT 制作** | 制作 Part 3 的 slides（约 12-15 页）；SPIN 的博弈示意图、Self-Rewarding vs RLHF 对比图 |\n| **内容研究** | 深读相关论文，特别理解 SPIN 的博弈论直觉和 Constitutional AI 的设计哲学 |\n| **互动设计** | 设计 FunSearch 相关的 Quiz 问题 |\n| **全场职责** | 负责收集和整理所有引用的 **参考文献列表**，放在最后的 References slide 中 |\n\n**关键论文**：\n- Chen et al., 2024 \"Self-Play Fine-Tuning Converts Weak Language Models to Strong Language Models\"\n- Yuan et al., 2024 \"Self-Rewarding Language Models\"\n- Bai et al., 2022 \"Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback\"\n- Romera-Paredes et al., 2024 \"Mathematical discoveries from program search with large language models\"（FunSearch）\n- Guo et al., 2023 \"Connecting Large Language Models with Evolutionary Algorithms Yields Powerful Prompt Optimizers\"（EvoPrompt）\n\n---\n\n### 成员 D：挑战、理论与未来（负责 Part 4）\n\n**演讲内容**：34:00 - 45:00（约 11 分钟）\n\n| 职责 | 具体任务 |\n|------|----------|\n| **演讲** | 讲解三大挑战、理论上界、前沿展望、全场总结；引导 Q&A |\n| **PPT 制作** | 制作 Part 4 的 slides（约 10-12 页）；Model Collapse 实验图、总结回顾 slide |\n| **内容研究** | 深读 Model Collapse、理论分析、Test-Time Compute 等相关论文 |\n| **Q&A 准备** | 提前准备 5-8 个可能的 Q&A 问题及简要回答，与全组共享 |\n| **全场职责** | 负责 **排练统筹**（组织至少 2 次完整排练，记录时间，确保不超时） |\n\n**关键论文**：\n- Shumailov et al., 2023 \"The Curse of Recursion: Training on Generated Data Makes Models Forget\"\n- Huang et al., 2022 \"Large Language Models Can Self-Improve\"\n- Snell et al., 2024 \"Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute\"\n- Hosseini et al., 2024 \"V-STaR: Training Verifiers for Self-Taught Reasoners\"\n\n---\n\n## 四、互动环节设计\n\n| 时间点 | 互动形式 | 负责人 | 说明 |\n|--------|----------|--------|------|\n| 0:00-1:30 | **举手投票** | 成员 A | \"你觉得训练一个 ChatGPT 级别的模型需要多少人工标注？\" |\n| 16:30-19:00 | **现场 Demo** | 成员 B | Self-Refine 演示：给模型一个任务，让它自我批评并改进 |\n| 31:00-34:00 | **Quiz 抢答** | 成员 C | FunSearch 相关的选择题，答对的同学可以获得小奖品（可选） |\n| 42:00-44:00 | **开放讨论** | 成员 D | 总结时的思考题，引导 Q&A |\n\n---\n\n## 五、PPT 结构建议\n\n```\nSlide 0   : 封面（标题、课程信息、组员信息）         [成员 A 制作]\nSlide 1-10: Part 1 - 动机与全景                       [成员 A 制作]\nSlide 11-25: Part 2 - 核心自我提升方法                 [成员 B 制作]\nSlide 26-40: Part 3 - 自我博弈与自我进化               [成员 C 制作]\nSlide 41-52: Part 4 - 挑战、展望与总结                 [成员 D 制作]\nSlide 53  : References                                 [成员 C 汇总]\nSlide 54  : Thank You / Q&A                            [成员 D 制作]\n```\n\n**视觉风格建议**：\n- 主色调：深蓝 + 白色 + 亮橙色（高亮重点用）\n- 字体：英文用 Helvetica/Arial，中文用思源黑体\n- 流程图风格统一，建议用同一套图标库\n- 每张 slide 的文字不超过 5 行，重点用图表和动画表达\n- 全景分类图在每个 Part 开始时复现，高亮当前讲解的部分（\"你在这里\"效果）\n\n---\n\n## 六、时间轴总览\n\n```\n 0:00 ──── 10:00 ──── 22:00 ──── 34:00 ──── 45:00\n │  成员 A  │  成员 B   │  成员 C   │  成员 D  │\n │  10 min  │  12 min   │  12 min   │  11 min  │\n │          │           │           │          │\n │ 动机     │ 核心方法   │ 博弈+进化  │ 挑战+未来 │\n │ +全景图  │ +Demo     │ +Quiz     │ +总结+QA │\n```\n\n---\n\n## 七、排练计划\n\n| 次数 | 时间建议 | 重点 |\n|------|----------|------|\n| **第 1 次** | 正式前 7-10 天 | 每人只讲大纲，确认内容覆盖无遗漏、无重复，调整时间分配 |\n| **第 2 次** | 正式前 3-4 天 | 完整 PPT 排练，严格计时，练习过渡语，测试 Demo |\n| **第 3 次**（可选）| 正式前 1 天 | 精简版快速过一遍，重点练薄弱环节和 Q&A 应对 |\n\n---\n\n## 八、风险与备案\n\n| 风险 | 备案 |\n|------|------|\n| Demo 现场翻车（网络问题/API 不响应） | 提前录好 Demo 视频 + 截图，可以无缝切换 |\n| 某位成员超时 | 每人 PPT 中标记\"可跳过\"的 slide，排练时练习压缩版 |\n| Q&A 冷场 | 成员 D 准备 2-3 个引导性问题（\"有同学想问 xxx 吗？\"） |\n| 观众对数学公式失去兴趣 | 所有公式都配直觉解释和类比，绝不出现\"这个公式很显然\" |\n\n---\n\n## 九、核心参考文献\n\n1. Wang et al., 2022. *Self-Instruct: Aligning Language Models with Self-Generated Instructions*\n2. Zelikman et al., 2022. *STaR: Bootstrapping Reasoning With Reasoning*\n3. Madaan et al., 2023. *Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback*\n4. Gulcehre et al., 2023. *Reinforced Self-Training (ReST) for Language Modeling*\n5. Chen et al., 2024. *Self-Play Fine-Tuning Converts Weak Language Models to Strong Language Models*\n6. Yuan et al., 2024. *Self-Rewarding Language Models*\n7. Bai et al., 2022. *Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback*\n8. Romera-Paredes et al., 2024. *Mathematical Discoveries from Program Search with Large Language Models* (FunSearch)\n9. Guo et al., 2023. *Connecting Large Language Models with Evolutionary Algorithms Yields Powerful Prompt Optimizers* (EvoPrompt)\n10. Shumailov et al., 2023. *The Curse of Recursion: Training on Generated Data Makes Models Forget*\n11. Huang et al., 2022. *Large Language Models Can Self-Improve*\n12. Snell et al., 2024. *Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute Optimally can be More Effective than Scaling Model Parameters*\n13. Singh et al., 2023. *Beyond Human Data: Scaling Self-Training for Problem-Solving with Language Models*\n14. Hosseini et al., 2024. *V-STaR: Training Verifiers for Self-Taught Reasoners*\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"c522d9a2-1f46-4c3f-aefc-f96f34cc9a87","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}

{"parentUuid":"c5a72654-2382-45e6-95ac-5a1a3694e297","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"9ec6ffc6-17fb-4d6f-9197-a90825d38591","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_015wVREWTwWnC5rKo3sctL1v","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /home/zhuyihan/.claude/projects/-home-zhuyihan-code-Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/memory/user_profile.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"uuid":"456c8dc0-b356-4c45-9700-e6f7cf1dfeaf","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:46:53.260Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"create","filePath":"/home/zhuyihan/.claude/projects/-home-zhuyihan-code-Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/memory/user_profile.md","content":"---\nname: user_profile\ndescription: SJTU ACM class sophomore taking CS2916 LLM course, working on group presentation about self-improving algorithms\ntype: user\noriginSessionId: 2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5\n---\n- 上海交通大学 ACM 班大二学生\n- 本学期修 CS2916 大语言模型课程\n- 正在准备一个 4 人小组 45 分钟 presentation，主题是自我提升与自我进化算法\n- 中文母语用户\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"c5a72654-2382-45e6-95ac-5a1a3694e297","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}

{"type":"file-history-snapshot","messageId":"badc582f-9d12-4b2b-82fe-f517e26dcb7c","snapshot":{"messageId":"ffc0eaef-d224-49b8-b93f-817643f6b243","trackedFileBackups":{"plan.md":{"backupFileName":null,"version":1,"backupTime":"2026-04-21T17:46:43.909Z"},"/home/zhuyihan/.claude/projects/-home-zhuyihan-code-Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/memory/user_profile.md":{"backupFileName":null,"version":1,"backupTime":"2026-04-21T17:46:53.252Z"},"/home/zhuyihan/.claude/projects/-home-zhuyihan-code-Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/memory/MEMORY.md":{"backupFileName":null,"version":1,"backupTime":"2026-04-21T17:46:54.809Z"}},"timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:43:19.582Z"},"isSnapshotUpdate":true}
{"parentUuid":"badc582f-9d12-4b2b-82fe-f517e26dcb7c","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"9ec6ffc6-17fb-4d6f-9197-a90825d38591","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01VsZwqzAuoYfdaLnzcBPUHe","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /home/zhuyihan/.claude/projects/-home-zhuyihan-code-Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/memory/MEMORY.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"uuid":"96d660de-73a2-452e-b627-c4c596936e31","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:46:54.850Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"create","filePath":"/home/zhuyihan/.claude/projects/-home-zhuyihan-code-Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/memory/MEMORY.md","content":"- [User Profile](user_profile.md) — SJTU ACM sophomore, CS2916 LLM course, group presentation on self-improving algorithms\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"badc582f-9d12-4b2b-82fe-f517e26dcb7c","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}
{"parentUuid":"96d660de-73a2-452e-b627-c4c596936e31","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_01NdjBYPizjAhJUhG8TJ1MPF","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"已经把完整的 presentation 计划写到 `plan.md` 中了。以下是核心要点概览：\n\n## 整体结构（45 分钟，4 人）\n\n| 成员 | 时间 | 主题 | 亮点 |\n|------|------|------|------|\n| **A** | 0-10 min | 动机与全景图 | 开场互动投票 + 全场复用的分类框架图 |\n| **B** | 10-22 min | 核心方法（Self-Instruct, STaR, Self-Refine, ReST） | 现场 Self-Refine Demo |\n| **C** | 22-34 min | 自我博弈与进化（SPIN, Self-Rewarding, Constitutional AI, FunSearch） | FunSearch Quiz 抢答 |\n| **D** | 34-45 min | 挑战、理论边界与未来展望 | 全场总结 + Q&A 引导 |\n\n## 核心叙事类比\n\n> LLM 的成长 = 学生从\"跟老师学\"到\"自己学\"。Pre-training 是读教科书，SFT/RLHF 是老师批作业，Self-Improving 是自己出题、自己做、自己批、自己改。\n\n## 设计亮点\n\n- **4 次互动**：投票、Demo、Quiz、开放讨论，保持观众注意力\n- **全景分类图**贯穿全场，每部分高亮当前位置，让观众始终知道\"我在哪\"\n- **幽默但不失严谨**：每个 Part 都有类比和金句，数学公式必配直觉解释\n- **风险备案**：Demo 翻车有录屏兜底，超时有可跳过 slide\n\n详细的分工、参考文献清单、PPT 结构、排练计划等都在 `plan.md` 中。"}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":537,"cache_read_input_tokens":39708,"output_tokens":548,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":537},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"type":"assistant","uuid":"d5e1dac6-56b4-41d6-8259-b9af1bf8cf7d","timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:47:08.718Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}
{"parentUuid":"d5e1dac6-56b4-41d6-8259-b9af1bf8cf7d","isSidechain":false,"type":"system","subtype":"turn_duration","durationMs":225401,"messageCount":23,"timestamp":"2026-04-21T17:47:08.812Z","uuid":"5f9da939-cd5a-407f-bdab-10b1951f1773","isMeta":false,"userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}

## Part A 演讲课件历史记录
{"parentUuid":"5f9da939-cd5a-407f-bdab-10b1951f1773","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"012b0117-a762-4327-8473-6968102bf3f9","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"我选择了A部分进行演讲，我希望你为我做一份html格式的课件，遵循你在plan.md中对A部分的要求，图片统一放到根目录下的assets文件夹里，如果有演示统一放到根目录下的demo文件夹里，希望精美漂亮一些，那么开始吧"},"uuid":"0127adc9-fefa-43bd-b233-158113618714","timestamp":"2026-04-21T18:07:05.405Z","permissionMode":"default","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}

{"type":"file-history-snapshot","messageId":"0127adc9-fefa-43bd-b233-158113618714","snapshot":{"messageId":"0127adc9-fefa-43bd-b233-158113618714","trackedFileBackups":{"/home/zhuyihan/.claude/projects/-home-zhuyihan-code-Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/memory/user_profile.md":{"backupFileName":"0d3c582bd4a5b252@v2","version":2,"backupTime":"2026-04-21T18:07:05.505Z"},"/home/zhuyihan/.claude/projects/-home-zhuyihan-code-Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/memory/MEMORY.md":{"backupFileName":"bf6f00f34bd7602f@v2","version":2,"backupTime":"2026-04-21T18:07:05.505Z"},"plan.md":{"backupFileName":"c8f7e5e509d77fe5@v2","version":2,"backupTime":"2026-04-21T18:07:05.505Z"}},"timestamp":"2026-04-21T18:07:05.506Z"},"isSnapshotUpdate":false}
{"parentUuid":"7226873b-bca1-4655-ae0a-40b6bedabef5","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_01NJ19mGJa4MEtSZa5KNcchm","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The user wants me to create an HTML presentation for Part A (the opening and background section) of their presentation. They want:\n\n1. HTML format slides\n2. Images in `/assets/` folder\n3. Demos in `/demo/` folder\n4. Beautiful and polished design\n\nLet me review what Part A covers from the plan:\n\nPart 1: 开场 — 为什么 LLM 需要\"自学\"？（成员 A，约 10 分钟）\n\n- 0:00-1:30: Hook - 互动投票：\"你觉得 GPT-4 级别的模型背后需要多少人工标注数据？\"\n\n\n- 1:30-3:30: The bottleneck section will cover the unsustainability of manual annotation, showing the cost curve for data labeling and how RLHF demands massive amounts of human preference data\n- 3:30-5:30: Then I'll introduce the bootstrap concept using a student self-learning analogy to explain the core idea\n- 5:30-8:00: A comprehensive framework that categorizes the different approaches\n- 8:00-10:00: Key questions to set up what's coming next, with a smooth transition to the next speaker\n\nI'll need to create an infographic showing the costs associated with human annotation to support this narrative.\n\nNow I'm building an interactive HTML presentation with a modern dark blue, white, and orange color scheme. I'll embed SVG graphics for the bootstrap intuition animation and the method taxonomy diagram, plus design interactive voting elements that let the audience participate. Building the presentation structure... Building the custom slide deck...\n\nI need to organize the assets properly—the user wants images in an assets folder, but for a self-contained presentation, I'll embed the interactive SVG diagrams directly in the HTML while saving static assets separately for cleaner file structure. I'll create SVG illustrations for the cost curve, bootstrap cycle, and panorama map, with a placeholder or stylized SVG for the Baron Munchausen reference since I can't generate actual images.\n\nNow I'm designing the visual style with a dark gradient background, bright orange and cyan accents, and semi-transparent card elements, then building out the complete slide deck with polished typography and layout.\n\nI'll consolidate everything into a single HTML file for portability, starting with the cover slide featuring the Chinese title and course information with subtle animations, followed by an interactive polling slide where users can click through multiple choice options about training data requirements.\n\nThen I'm mapping out the reveal slides showing InstructGPT's actual annotation costs and the three major bottlenecks—expense, quality inconsistency, and scarcity of qualified annotators—before moving into the humorous \"where do we find 100,000 math PhDs\" question with supporting visuals.\n\nNow I'm introducing the self-bootstrapping concept through the Munchausen metaphor, showing how models can train on their own outputs, then illustrating this with a student's learning journey from textbooks through teacher feedback to self-directed improvement. The panorama map slide will be my central reference framework organizing all the methods into three categories, and I'm setting up key transition questions to guide the narrative forward.\n\nBuilding the HTML presentation... Writing the HTML structure... Writing the HTML file... I'm settling on a dark, modern color scheme with deep blue backgrounds, light text, and vibrant accent colors in orange, cyan, and green—perfect for a tech-focused presentation. Now I'll build out the HTML with smooth fade and slide transitions for the slides, plus staggered fade-in animations for individual elements.\n\nFor the interactive components, I'm planning animated bar charts that grow from zero and SVG paths that draw themselves using stroke animations, all handled with pure CSS. The poll will have clickable option cards that reveal results when selected, and I'll add keyboard shortcuts for fullscreen, a progress bar, and click-to-navigate functionality.\n\nI'm going to create a comprehensive HTML file as the main presentation, then save reusable SVG assets separately—specifically the annotation cost infographic, a bootstrap intuition animation, and the methodology taxonomy diagram that carries through the entire talk.\n\nSince the taxonomy map is crucial for all team members to reference and highlight their respective sections, I should build that as a standalone SVG asset. Now I'm mapping out the slide structure, starting with the cover slide introducing self-improvement algorithms in LLMs, followed by a hook slide to draw the audience in.\n\nFor the interactive poll, I'm asking about the scale of human-annotated data needed to train models like GPT-4, with options ranging from thousands to millions of data points. Then I'll reveal the answer with concrete examples—InstructGPT's annotation scale, RLHF costs, and GPT-4's reported data requirements—to show just how massive the actual numbers are.\n\nNow I'm breaking down the core bottlenecks: the astronomical costs per annotation, the shortage of qualified domain experts across different fields, and the consistency problems that create noisy data. I'm visualizing this with a cost curve showing exponential scaling, then hitting the audience with a rhetorical question about where to find hundreds of thousands of qualified annotators who can work quickly and accurately without burning out.\n\nThat leads into the key pivot—what if the model could improve itself? I'm introducing the bootstrap concept through the Munchausen story as a metaphor: just as the Baron pulled himself out by his own bootstraps, models can extract signal from their own outputs to iteratively improve. It sounds circular in theory, but under the right conditions it actually works. Then I'm setting up the student analogy to show how LLMs progress through different learning stages, starting with pre-training as the foundational reading phase.\n\nNow I'm laying out whether Stage 3—true self-improvement—is actually feasible, and mapping out the full taxonomy of approaches: self-improving methods like Self-Instruct and STaR, self-play techniques like SPIN, and self-evolving systems like Constitutional AI.\n\nI'm framing the core tension for the audience with three key questions: can a model really strengthen itself through self-training, isn't this just self-grading, and where are the limits? Then handing off to the next presenter to dive into the specifics.\n\nNow I'm building the presentation deck...\n\nBuilding the interactive presentation... I'm setting up the HTML structure with slides that support fragment animations, where pressing next reveals hidden elements before advancing to the next slide. Now I'm designing the panorama map diagram with a hierarchical layout showing the relationships between different components of the self-improvement algorithm. Building the presentation with premium design elements...\n\nI'm going to structure this as one comprehensive HTML file with inline CSS and JavaScript to keep it self-contained. The panorama map will use HTML and CSS with connecting lines rather than SVG for better responsiveness and animation control. I'll aim for around 900 lines total across styles, markup, and interactivity to keep everything organized while delivering a technically impressive presentation for these top CS students.\n\nFor the interactive poll, I'm planning to make it really engaging with clickable option cards that highlight on selection and trigger a result animation afterward.\n\nI'll pre-populate the bar chart with realistic vote percentages that show the audience mostly got it right, which creates a satisfying moment.\n\nNow I'm ready to build the full presentation as one comprehensive HTML file, using modern CSS features like Grid, custom properties, animations, and glassmorphism effects.\n\nWriting the complete file... Writing the HTML structure... Writing the HTML structure... Writing the HTML presentation...\n\nI'll set up the fonts with a link tag instead of @import to avoid rendering delays, using Inter for the main content and Noto Sans SC for Chinese characters with solid system font fallbacks. Then I'm creating the assets folder with a panorama classification map SVG and a demo folder with an interactive Self-Refine process visualization. I'm settling on the final structure now—the main HTML file will contain all the slides with inline CSS and JavaScript, the assets folder holds the SVG diagrams, and the demo folder stays empty for Part A. Before I start writing, I want to nail down the Chinese humor and tone to make it resonate with the SJTU ACM audience, thinking through some specific jokes about data labeling challenges and classic paradoxes.\n\nWriting the presentation code now... I'm going with full viewport for simplicity and maximum impact. Now I'm actually writing the code—no more deliberating. I'll create the directories, then write out the main HTML file with the panorama SVG asset, keeping everything inline and focused on quality. For the 16:9 presentation format, each slide will have a title area at the top and content area below.\n\nWriting the HTML file... Building the slide structure...\n\nI'm mapping out the presentation flow—starting with a cover slide for Shanghai Jiao Tong University's spring 2026 session, then moving into an engaging hook with a poll to get the audience thinking about data requirements for training large language models. After revealing the answer, I'll walk through the key bottlenecks and pivot points, structuring everything across roughly 11 slides for the first 10-minute segment. Rather than overthinking the details, I'm just going to start writing the code and let the structure come together naturally.\n\nFor the visual design, I'm leaning toward a subtle grid pattern on a dark background to give it a technical feel without being too distracting, maybe with a few floating elements for visual interest. Building the background styling now...\n\nI'm realizing this HTML file could get quite large with all the content and styling, so I should think about whether to keep it as one file or split it into separate parts to keep things manageable.\n\nActually, keeping it as a single file makes more sense for portability—presentations are easier to share when everything's self-contained. I'll organize it with clear comments to keep the structure readable.\n\nNow I'm focusing on which slides need the most visual polish: the cover slide for impact, the interactive poll, the bottleneck visualization with the cost breakdown, and the panorama map as a key reference point. Let me prioritize getting those right.\n\nWriting the complete HTML presentation now. I'm estimating around 1100 lines total—roughly 400 for styles with animations, 500 for the slide content, and 200 for the navigation and interaction logic. I'll build in the poll interaction with click handlers that highlight selections and animate the results bars appearing.\n\nSetting up the design system with CSS variables for a dark, modern aesthetic using slate and accent colors, then structuring the HTML slides, and finally adding the JavaScript for smooth interactions. Writing the complete file...\n\nI'm thinking through the UI layout for the bottom of the slides—a subtle progress bar, slide counter in the bottom right, and navigation hints on the left so viewers know how to move through the presentation. I should also decide what goes in the assets and demo folders; I'll put the panorama map SVG in assets for reference and keep the demo folder ready even if it's not essential for Part A.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":41062,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":12199,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":41062},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"type":"assistant","uuid":"bb53134a-be94-4122-9456-ef6380c69c6a","timestamp":"2026-04-21T18:11:07.046Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}

{"parentUuid":"78cb2601-433b-4a97-afa6-621cb4cb70ec","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"012b0117-a762-4327-8473-6968102bf3f9","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01U9o9bBP96EfsqqmF2UPs35","type":"tool_result","content":"Updated task #1 status"}]},"uuid":"4dafe2e8-c673-41e1-97f5-85fc436a2365","timestamp":"2026-04-21T18:12:11.333Z","toolUseResult":{"success":true,"taskId":"1","updatedFields":["status"],"statusChange":{"from":"in_progress","to":"completed"},"verificationNudgeNeeded":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"78cb2601-433b-4a97-afa6-621cb4cb70ec","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}
{"parentUuid":"4dafe2e8-c673-41e1-97f5-85fc436a2365","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_017yw1riHK5Qm1gSEFdzsi4s","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_bdrk_01HZ3sFcnEff3vrtRecK7Db4","name":"TaskUpdate","input":{"status":"in_progress","taskId":"2"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":88,"cache_read_input_tokens":53584,"output_tokens":72,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":88},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"type":"assistant","uuid":"3b19012c-ab7f-4dd4-b9eb-fbde0eeb5945","timestamp":"2026-04-21T18:12:15.562Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}
{"parentUuid":"3b19012c-ab7f-4dd4-b9eb-fbde0eeb5945","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"012b0117-a762-4327-8473-6968102bf3f9","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01HZ3sFcnEff3vrtRecK7Db4","type":"tool_result","content":"Updated task #2 status"}]},"uuid":"974aa94c-4c0a-4fc3-b72d-ffcbbb102025","timestamp":"2026-04-21T18:12:15.589Z","toolUseResult":{"success":true,"taskId":"2","updatedFields":["status"],"statusChange":{"from":"pending","to":"in_progress"},"verificationNudgeNeeded":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"3b19012c-ab7f-4dd4-b9eb-fbde0eeb5945","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}
{"parentUuid":"974aa94c-4c0a-4fc3-b72d-ffcbbb102025","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_01NtgCGdE1PuJkwGYYpEZz4m","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now let me build the main presentation."}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":90,"cache_read_input_tokens":53672,"output_tokens":20102,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":90},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"type":"assistant","uuid":"3e8951c1-e87e-4bd0-9ffc-4fcfad7a3b7d","timestamp":"2026-04-21T18:12:21.390Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm","sessionId":"2ec180f5-45c4-4275-ac33-963f920665e5","version":"2.1.109","gitBranch":"main","slug":"indexed-percolating-marshmallow"}
{"parentUuid":"3e8951c1-e87e-4bd0-9ffc-4fcfad7a3b7d","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_bdrk_01NtgCGdE1PuJkwGYYpEZz4m","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_bdrk_013XEjDPQexW5dBySK19cm6m","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/zhuyihan/code/Self-Improving-Evolving-Algorithm/slides_part_a.html","content":"<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"zh-CN\">\n<head>\n  <meta charset=\"UTF-8\">\n  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\">\n  <title>自我提升与自我进化算法 - Part A</title>\n  <link rel=\"preconnect\" href=\"https://fonts.googleapis.com\">\n  <link href=\"https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700;800;900&family=Noto+Sans+SC:wght@300;400;500;700;900&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500&display=swap\" rel=\"stylesheet\">\n  <style>\n    /* ============================================================\n       CSS Variables & Reset\n       ============================================================ */\n    :root {\n      --bg-primary: #0a0f1e;\n      --bg-secondary: #111827;\n      --bg-card: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.04);\n      --bg-card-hover: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08);\n      --text-primary: #f1f5f9;\n      --text-secondary: #94a3b8;\n      --text-muted: #64748b;\n      --accent-orange: #f97316;\n      --accent-orange-glow: rgba(249, 115, 22, 0.3);\n      --accent-cyan: #06b6d4;\n      --accent-cyan-glow: rgba(6, 182, 212, 0.3);\n      --accent-green: #22c55e;\n      --accent-green-glow: rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.3);\n      --accent-purple: #a855f7;\n      --accent-red: #ef4444;\n      --border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08);\n      --border-light: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.15);\n      --radius: 16px;\n      --radius-sm: 10px;\n      --shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3);\n      --font-sans: 'Inter', 'Noto Sans SC', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'PingFang SC', 'Microsoft YaHei', sans-serif;\n      --font-mono: 'JetBrains Mono', 'SF Mono', 'Fira Code', Consolas, monospace;\n    }\n\n    *, *::before, *::after { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }\n\n    html, body {\n      width: 100%; height: 100%;\n      font-family: var(--font-sans);\n      background: var(--bg-primary);\n      color: var(--text-primary);\n      overflow: hidden;\n      -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;\n      -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale;\n    }\n\n    /* ============================================================\n       Slide Framework\n       ============================================================ */\n    .presentation {\n      width: 100%; height: 100%;\n      position: relative;\n    }\n\n    .slide {\n      position: absolute;\n      inset: 0;\n      display: flex;\n      flex-direction: column;\n      justify-content: center;\n      align-items: center;\n      padding: 50px 80px;\n      opacity: 0;\n      transform: translateX(40px) scale(0.98);\n      transition: opacity 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1),\n                  transform 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);\n      pointer-events: none;\n      z-index: 0;\n      background:\n        radial-gradient(ellipse at 20% 50%, rgba(6, 182, 212, 0.03) 0%, transparent 50%),\n        radial-gradient(ellipse at 80% 50%, rgba(249, 115, 22, 0.03) 0%, transparent 50%),\n        linear-gradient(180deg, var(--bg-primary) 0%, #0d1525 100%);\n    }\n\n    .slide::before {\n      content: '';\n      position: absolute; inset: 0;\n      background:\n        linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(255,255,255,0.015) 1px, transparent 1px),\n        linear-gradient(rgba(255,255,255,0.015) 1px, transparent 1px);\n      background-size: 60px 60px;\n      pointer-events: none;\n    }\n\n    .slide.active {\n      opacity: 1;\n      transform: translateX(0) scale(1);\n      pointer-events: all;\n      z-index: 1;\n    }\n\n    .slide.exit-left {\n      opacity: 0;\n      transform: translateX(-40px) scale(0.98);\n    }\n\n    .slide-content {\n      position: relative;\n      z-index: 1;\n      width: 100%;\n      max-width: 1100px;\n      text-align: center;\n    }\n\n    /* ============================================================\n       Fragments (step-by-step reveal within a slide)\n       ============================================================ */\n    .fragment {\n      opacity: 0;\n      transform: translateY(20px);\n      transition: opacity 0.5s ease, transform 0.5s ease;\n    }\n\n    .fragment.visible {\n      opacity: 1;\n      transform: translateY(0);\n    }\n\n    /* ============================================================\n       Typography\n       ============================================================ */\n    h1 {\n      font-size: clamp(42px, 5vw, 72px);\n      font-weight: 800;\n      line-height: 1.15;\n      letter-spacing: -0.02em;\n      margin-bottom: 16px;\n    }\n\n    h2 {\n      font-size: clamp(28px, 3.5vw, 48px);\n      font-weight: 700;\n      line-height: 1.25;\n      letter-spacing: -0.01em;\n      margin-bottom: 20px;\n    }\n\n    h3 {\n      font-size: clamp(20px, 2.5vw, 32px);\n      font-weight: 600;\n      line-height: 1.3;\n      margin-bottom: 12px;\n    }\n\n    .subtitle {\n      font-size: clamp(18px, 2vw, 28px);\n      color: var(--text-secondary);\n      font-weight: 400;\n      line-height: 1.5;\n    }\n\n    .big-text {\n      font-size: clamp(32px, 4vw, 56px);\n      font-weight: 700;\n      line-height: 1.3;\n    }\n\n    .gradient-text {\n      background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--accent-orange) 0%, var(--accent-cyan) 100%);\n      -webkit-background-clip: text;\n      -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent;\n      background-clip: text;\n    }\n\n    .gradient-text-reverse {\n      background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--accent-cyan) 0%, var(--accent-green) 100%);\n      -webkit-background-clip: text;\n      -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent;\n      background-clip: text;\n    }\n\n    .highlight-orange { color: var(--accent-orange); }\n    .highlight-cyan { color: var(--accent-cyan); }\n    .highlight-green { color: var(--accent-green); }\n    .highlight-purple { color: var(--accent-purple); }\n\n    .tag {\n      display: inline-block;\n      padding: 6px 18px;\n      background: var(--bg-card);\n      border: 1px solid var(--border-light);\n      border-radius: 999px;\n      font-size: 14px;\n      font-weight: 500;\n      color: var(--text-secondary);\n      letter-spacing: 0.05em;\n      text-transform: uppercase;\n      margin-bottom: 24px;\n    }\n\n    .quote {\n      font-size: clamp(20px, 2.5vw, 30px);\n      font-style: italic;\n      color: var(--text-secondary);\n      border-left: 3px solid var(--accent-orange);\n      padding-left: 24px;\n      text-align: left;\n      max-width: 800px;\n      margin: 24px auto;\n      line-height: 1.6;\n    }\n\n    /* ============================================================\n       Cards & Containers\n       ============================================================ */\n    .glass-card {\n      background: var(--bg-card);\n      backdrop-filter: blur(12px);\n      -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(12px);\n      border: 1px solid var(--border);\n      border-radius: var(--radius);\n      padding: 32px;\n      transition: all 0.3s ease;\n    }\n\n    .glass-card:hover {\n      background: var(--bg-card-hover);\n      border-color: var(--border-light);\n      transform: translateY(-2px);\n      box-shadow: var(--shadow);\n    }\n\n    .grid-2 {\n      display: grid;\n      grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;\n      gap: 24px;\n      width: 100%;\n    }\n\n    .grid-3 {\n      display: grid;\n      grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr;\n      gap: 24px;\n      width: 100%;\n    }\n\n    .grid-2x2 {\n      display: grid;\n      grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;\n      grid-template-rows: 1fr 1fr;\n      gap: 20px;\n      width: 100%;\n      max-width: 900px;\n    }\n\n    /* ============================================================\n       Cover Slide Specifics\n       ============================================================ */\n    #cover {\n      background:\n        radial-gradient(ellipse at 30% 20%, rgba(249, 115, 22, 0.08) 0%, transparent 50%),\n        radial-gradient(ellipse at 70% 80%, rgba(6, 182, 212, 0.08) 0%, transparent 50%),\n        radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 50%, rgba(168, 85, 247, 0.05) 0%, transparent 60%),\n        linear-gradient(180deg, #080d1a 0%, #0f172a 100%);\n    }\n\n    #cover::before {\n      background:\n        linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(255,255,255,0.02) 1px, transparent 1px),\n        linear-gradient(rgba(255,255,255,0.02) 1px, transparent 1px);\n      background-size: 80px 80px;\n    }\n\n    .cover-orbs {\n      position: absolute; inset: 0; overflow: hidden; pointer-events: none;\n    }\n\n    .cover-orbs .orb {\n      position: absolute;\n      border-radius: 50%;\n      filter: blur(80px);\n      animation: float 8s ease-in-out infinite;\n    }\n\n    .cover-orbs .orb:nth-child(1) {\n      width: 400px; height: 400px;\n      background: rgba(249, 115, 22, 0.12);\n      top: -10%; left: 10%;\n      animation-delay: 0s;\n    }\n\n    .cover-orbs .orb:nth-child(2) {\n      width: 350px; height: 350px;\n      background: rgba(6, 182, 212, 0.12);\n      bottom: -10%; right: 10%;\n      animation-delay: -3s;\n    }\n\n    .cover-orbs .orb:nth-child(3) {\n      width: 250px; height: 250px;\n      background: rgba(168, 85, 247, 0.08);\n      top: 40%; right: 30%;\n      animation-delay: -5s;\n    }\n\n    @keyframes float {\n      0%, 100% { transform: translate(0, 0) scale(1); }\n      33% { transform: translate(20px, -20px) scale(1.05); }\n      66% { transform: translate(-15px, 15px) scale(0.95); }\n    }\n\n    .cover-meta {\n      display: flex;\n      gap: 32px;\n      justify-content: center;\n      margin-top: 40px;\n      flex-wrap: wrap;\n    }\n\n    .cover-meta span {\n      font-size: 15px;\n      color: var(--text-muted);\n      font-weight: 400;\n    }\n\n    .cover-meta span::before {\n      content: '';\n      display: inline-block;\n      width: 6px; height: 6px;\n      border-radius: 50%;\n      background: var(--accent-orange);\n      margin-right: 10px;\n      vertical-align: middle;\n    }\n\n    .cover-divider {\n      width: 80px; height: 3px;\n      background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--accent-orange), var(--accent-cyan));\n      border-radius: 2px;\n      margin: 32px auto;\n    }\n\n    /* ============================================================\n       Poll Slide\n       ============================================================ */\n    .poll-option {\n      background: var(--bg-card);\n      border: 2px solid var(--border);\n      border-radius: var(--radius);\n      padding: 24px 28px;\n      cursor: pointer;\n      transition: all 0.3s ease;\n      text-align: left;\n      position: relative;\n      overflow: hidden;\n    }\n\n    .poll-option::before {\n      content: '';\n      position: absolute;\n      inset: 0;\n      background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--accent-orange-glow), transparent);\n      opacity: 0;\n      transition: opacity 0.3s ease;\n    }\n\n    .poll-option:hover {\n      border-color: var(--accent-orange);\n      transform: translateY(-3px);\n      box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(249, 115, 22, 0.15);\n    }\n\n    .poll-option:hover::before { opacity: 1; }\n\n    .poll-option.selected {\n      border-color: var(--accent-orange);\n      background: rgba(249, 115, 22, 0.1);\n    }\n\n    .poll-option.correct {\n      border-color: var(--accent-green) !important;\n      background: rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.1) !important;\n      box-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.2) !important;\n    }\n\n    .poll-option .letter {\n      position: relative;\n      display: inline-flex;\n      align-items: center;\n      justify-content: center;\n      width: 36px; height: 36px;\n      border-radius: 10px;\n      background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08);\n      font-weight: 700;\n      font-size: 16px;\n      color: var(--accent-orange);\n      margin-right: 16px;\n      flex-shrink: 0;\n    }\n\n    .poll-option .option-text {\n      position: relative;\n    }\n\n    .poll-option .option-main {\n      font-size: 20px;\n      font-weight: 600;\n      display: block;\n      margin-bottom: 4px;\n    }\n\n    .poll-option .option-desc {\n      font-size: 14px;\n      color: var(--text-muted);\n      font-weight: 400;\n    }\n\n    .poll-option .option-row {\n      display: flex;\n      align-items: center;\n    }\n\n    /* ============================================================\n       Result Bar Chart\n       ============================================================ */\n    .result-section {\n      margin-top: 32px;\n      display: none;\n    }\n\n    .result-section.show { display: block; }\n\n    .result-bar-group {\n      display: flex;\n      align-items: center;\n      margin-bottom: 14px;\n    }\n\n    .result-label {\n      width: 80px;\n      font-size: 14px;\n      font-weight: 600;\n      color: var(--text-secondary);\n      flex-shrink: 0;\n    }\n\n    .result-bar-track {\n      flex: 1;\n      height: 32px;\n      background: rgba(255,255,255,0.04);\n      border-radius: 8px;\n      overflow: hidden;\n      position: relative;\n    }\n\n    .result-bar-fill {\n      height: 100%;\n
